0x41434f

0x41434f

I am a software engineer and mental health worker building toward a career in Computational Psychiatry and Health Policy.

I am currently finishing my medical school prerequisites with the goal of becoming a Consultation-Liaison Psychiatrist. My background includes a BS in Computer Science with a Cybersecurity focus, 7+ years as an engineer and venture capital analyst, and hundreds of hours on the floor of an acute psychiatric hospital.

I am the founder of Stratification Labs, where we build Psykick and Psychify to reduce administrative burden and end trial-and-error psychiatry.

Essays

These essays document my journey from a personal crisis to a new mission in mental health. They are best read in order.

  • May 28, 2025 — What I'm Learning About Stability
    On stress, sleep, and the man at Panera Bread whose life showed me how short the distance is between stability and crisis.
  • September 9, 2025 — My World Fell Apart This Summer
    My worst fears happened. I found calm in the middle of it. That calm sent me searching for answers psychiatry does not yet have.
  • October 9, 2025 — My First Two Weeks on a Psychiatric Floor
    I stepped onto the floor knowing almost nothing. Two weeks later, I am certain this is the most important work I can do.
  • October 10, 2025 — Understanding Involuntary Holds
    The legal codes tell us when we can hold someone. They do not tell us what to do with them once we do. That gap is where patients get lost.
  • October 18, 2025 — Can I Speak to My Social Worker
    The most common request on the psychiatric floor is not for medication or a doctor. It is for the one person who can answer: what happens to me when I leave?
  • November 1, 2025 — 792 Hours in Acute Psychiatric Hospital
    After 33 days on the floor, the systems failures are no longer abstract. We are managing risk. We are not yet healing people.
  • November 15, 2025 — Why Psychiatry Needs Stratification
    After 62 days on the floor, I thought psychiatry was fake. Then I found a hospital that was already doing it right.
  • November 24, 2025 — The Entropy Trap
    The psychiatric unit is not a therapeutic environment. It is a stochastic noise generator designed to maximize uncertainty.
  • November 29, 2025 — The Ontological Error
    California SB 331 proposes to let a private committee's symptom checklist define when a citizen loses their freedom. This is a category error.
  • December 2, 2025 — My First Time In An Ambulance
    The ER found a life-threatening clot invisible to the naked eye. Back on the psych floor, we have no sensors at all.
  • December 15, 2025 — The Stratification Mandate
    What Stratification Labs believes, what we build, and what we expect from anyone who wants to work with us.
  • January 15, 2026 — The Machine Doesn't Know
    A patient I knew died with a CPAP mask on their face. The machine kept running. Every protocol that exists now could have existed before.
  • February 15, 2026 — The Policy Floor
    What surprised me was not how broken the system is. It was how precisely the incentives are designed to produce exactly the outcomes I see every night.
  • March 13, 2026 — What the APA Leadership Summit Taught Me
    Three days in Montgomery validated what months on the floor had been telling me. The technology and the policy have to move together.

Feel free to reach out if you want to talk about mental health policy, computational psychiatry, or what it is like to work the floor of a psychiatric hospital.

Connect

© 2026 0x414344f

Software Engineer. Mental Health Worker. Building toward Computational Psychiatry.
